_meta.authority: Read directly from the Hunt-tile face scans in the TTS mod (each tile is a scan of the real component). Tile MULTIPLICITIES come from counting how many physical tile objects share each face texture; they reconcile EXACTLY to 16 standard + 8 special = 24 base tiles. Cross-checked vs rulebook p.40-42: red Shadow specials all carry the Stop icon (confirmed), blue Fellowship specials are 0/negative (confirmed).
_meta.source: TTS mod 1831369203 Hunt Tile objects (DiffuseURL textures); not committed.

model.value: number 0..3 = Hunt damage. 'eye' = damage equals the number of Hunt successes rolled (0 if drawn for a reveal/Stronghold, not a roll). 'die' = roll a die for damage. negative = SUBTRACT from Corruption (min 0).
model.reveal: true if the tile has the Reveal icon (Fellowship is revealed after other effects).
model.stop: Shadow special only: the Mordor 'Stop' icon — on the Mordor Track the Fellowship does not advance a step.

_meta.engineUse: The 16 standard tiles are the Hunt Pool (rules-spec §10). Specials are set aside and enter only via Event cards, then join the pool when the Fellowship enters Mordor. Draw via Rng; when the pool empties, return STANDARD tiles only.
verify: Exact STANDARD multiset (esp. Eye x4, '3' x3) — reconciles to 16 but confirm against an official tile list; the mod is a fan creation.

Blue Fellowship special ICONS/effects: tile with a gold RING (x2) and a tile with a horn/mallet icon (x1) — exact effect text unconfirmed; resolve when cataloguing the Event cards that introduce each special tile.

specialFellowship.note: blue; gold One Ring icon; effect unconfirmed
